section.title: Development
section.subtitle: Software, Prototypes, and Research Tools
section.description: Practical systems, prototypes, dashboards, and reusable tools that translate HUMACH research ideas into usable software, student training outputs, and collaborative technology assets.

items.title: Research Dashboard Platforms
items.summary: Interactive web interfaces for managing datasets, experiments, metrics, papers, team outputs, and project progress.
items.details: These systems help research teams move from scattered files and spreadsheets to structured, visual, and reproducible research management.

items.title: AI Prototype Systems
items.summary: Applied AI models, assistants, decision-support workflows, and intelligent interfaces for research and real-world use cases.
items.details: Prototype work converts machine learning, NLP, generative AI, and decision-support ideas into demonstrable systems.

items.title: Data Processing Pipelines
items.summary: Reusable workflows for data cleaning, feature extraction, validation, model evaluation, and automated reporting.
items.details: Pipeline development supports reproducibility, repeated experiments, and reliable comparison across projects and datasets.

items.title: Open Research Tools
items.summary: Reusable templates, scripts, packages, documentation, and GitHub-ready codebases for students and collaborators.
items.details: Open tools make HUMACH outputs easier to reuse, audit, extend, teach, and showcase in student portfolios.

items.title: Health Technology Applications
items.summary: Interfaces and tools for biomedical signal analysis, sleep research, clinical data exploration, and health-focused AI communication.
items.details: This direction links HUMACH's health research identity with usable software systems for analysis, education, and decision support.

items.title: Education and Training Platforms
items.summary: Learning pages, guided tasks, internship dashboards, and training resources for research and software development pathways.
items.details: Training platforms help students move from basic learning to project participation, documentation, and portfolio-ready outputs.
